title: Hybrid Architectures
concept: Designing systems where a single agent delegates specific sub-tasks to short-lived sub-agent threads.

exercises.prompt: What should you do when a single agent's tasks become too complicated?

options.text: Keep adding more rules to the agent
Divide tasks into sub-tasks for efficiency
Ignore the complications and continue as is

exercises.explain: You should divide tasks into sub-tasks for efficiency, as this helps manage complexity.

cards.title: Hybrid Architectures
cards.body: Hybrid architectures involve one main agent that delegates specific tasks to smaller sub-agents. This setup enhances efficiency and adaptability.

cards.title: What is a Hybrid Architecture?
cards.body: A hybrid architecture is a system design where a primary agent controls several temporary sub-agents that perform specific tasks.

cards.title: Like a Team Project
cards.body: Imagine a project team where one leader assigns different tasks to team members. Each member focuses only on their task, making the project run smoothly.

cards.title: Example of Hybrid Architecture
cards.body: In an online event, a main AI manages registrations, while sub-agents handle tasks like sending confirmations and managing questions.

cards.title: Myth: More agents mean more confusion
cards.body: It's a myth that having more agents complicates things. When well-organized, sub-agents can streamline tasks.

cards.title: Visual Comparison
cards.body: This diagram shows how a main agent interacts with sub-agents to handle tasks effectively.

right.text: Perform specific short-lived tasks as needed.

exercises.prompt: In a hybrid architecture, what role does the main agent play?

options.text: Creates random tasks for others to do

exercises.explain: The main agent delegates specific tasks to sub-agents, ensuring the system works more effectively.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Sub-agents in a hybrid architecture can only work independently without guidance.
exercises.explain: This statement is false because sub-agents require direction from the main agent.

exercises.prompt: In a hybrid architecture, the primary agent ______ specific tasks to sub-agents.
exercises.sentence: In a hybrid architecture, the primary agent delegates specific tasks to sub-agents.
exercises.explain: The primary agent delegates specific tasks to ensure efficient task management.

exercises.prompt: Which option best describes the function of sub-agents in a hybrid architecture?

options.text: They only manage their own tasks without any communication with the main agent.
They assist the primary agent by handling specific short-term tasks.
They replace the primary agent completely in complex scenarios.

exercises.explain: They assist the primary agent by handling specific short-term tasks, allowing for improved efficiency.

exercises.prompt: What is one key benefit of using hybrid architectures in AI design?

options.text: They limit the number of agents used in a project.
They allow a single agent to efficiently manage multiple tasks by using sub-agents.
They are easier to understand but less effective than single-agent systems.
They increase the chances of conflicting decisions among agents.

exercises.explain: The key benefit of hybrid architectures is that they allow a single agent to efficiently manage multiple tasks by using sub-agents, enhancing overall performance and flexibility.
